task_completion.description: Configure the project dependencies to include Dagger Hilt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing build.gradle or build.gradle.kts files open in Android Studio with Hilt plugins (e.g., 'com.google.dagger.hilt.android') and dependencies (e.g., 'hilt-android', 'hilt-compiler') added.

task_completion.description: Set up the Hilt application class by annotating ExpenseApp.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ExpenseApp.kt open in the Android Studio editor, showing the class annotated with '@HiltAndroidApp'.

task_completion.description: Create a Hilt module (AppModule) to provide the required services.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named AppModule.kt open in the editor, annotated with '@Module' and '@InstallIn(SingletonComponent::class)', containing '@Provides' functions for CurrencyService, ExpenseRepository, NotificationService, and SettingsManager.

task_completion.description: Implement field injection in MainActivity, AddExpenseActivity, and SettingsActivity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Activity files (MainActivity.kt, AddExpenseActivity.kt, SettingsActivity.kt) annotated with '@AndroidEntryPoint', and their dependencies (CurrencyService, ExpenseRepository, etc.) declared with '@Inject lateinit var' instead of manual instantiation.

task_completion.description: Ensure the project compiles successfully after the refactor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Build' tool window at the bottom of Android Studio showing a 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' message or a green checkmark indicating the Gradle build finished without errors.

integrity.description: The agent used the Android Studio GUI to edit files and build the project.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Android Studio IDE interface is visible with code open in the editor tabs and the Gradle build panel used, rather than just terminal text editors (like nano/vim) or echo commands.

integrity.description: The agent modified the actual Kotlin files within the project structure.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Project tool window in Android Studio shows the correct path (app/src/main/java/com/example/expensetracker) and the editor shows syntax highlighting and valid Kotlin code.

integrity.description: The build success message is genuine and not a mocked terminal output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' message appears inside the standard Android Studio Build/Run tool window with associated Gradle task execution logs, not just printed in a plain terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/ExpenseTrackerApp with package com.example.expensetracker. The three activities that require @AndroidEntryPoint and @Inject are MainActivity, AddExpenseActivity, and SettingsActivity. The AppModule must provide CurrencyService, ExpenseRepository, NotificationService, and SettingsManager. Originally, these were instantiated manually (e.g., CurrencyService.getInstance()); the refactor must replace these manual calls with Hilt dependency injection.
